creator: adamw@adamw.org
text:
On 7 May 2014, at 0:34, Xin Li wrote:

> I think it's better to separate data/ tree out from where scripts are:
> on my web server, my dynamic contents interpreters are configured to
> never run the data directory, which is located outside of their roots.

You are 100% correct, that is absolutely best-practice. I think your 
intention is good, making it harder for people to shoot themselves in 
the foot.

However, that is a decision that is up to each admin. Currently, the 
port produces a non-functional installation out of the box. By following 
the instructions in the pkg-message, you get an installation that (a) 
doesn't work, and (b) breaks on every upgrade. That really does need to 
be changed.

To me, there are really three options here:
1) Have a functional installation out-of-the-box, so that a new admin 
can simply go, or can cp -Rp the data directory to the new location. The 
pkg-message could be altered to recommend putting it in a different 
place (mail/squirrelmail takes that approach, for example). This way, 
everybody wins... the port always works for everybody, and you've gone 
above-and-beyond to advise people in best-practice behaviour.

2) Alter the pkg-message to say that the port is intentionally 
non-usable in its default state. Say that using the default installation 
is unsupported and tell them exactly what to do instead. And probably, 
add a script to help them do it.

3) Create the data directory structure somewhere better by default (with 
correct permissions though!), and let the port user set a variable to 
specify where that is.

> Also, why data/pages/wiki, data/pages/playground,
> data/media/wiki and data are singled out from being owned by WWW 
> owner?

dokuwiki didn't complain about permissions on those directories, though 
I suspect you're right and everything under data/ needs to be owned by 
www:www. That said, I'm nearly positive that data/ itself shouldn't be 
owned by www.

# Adam


--
Adam Weinberger
adamw@adamw.org
http://www.adamw.org
